-- 3 of 9 --

Upsells
- Reverse engineer your potential upsells for your
front-end product before you ever create that
front-end product
- Do NOT just put out an offer and then say “ok now
what can we sell on the upsell”
- If a product does NOT have a good, natural upsell, I
would not create that product (no matter how good it
is)
Examples of good, natural upsells
- is there a natural upsell that will sell well with this
product? If yes, then create that product.
Upsells that always work…
- More of the same
- Do the work for them
- Get them results faster
- survival food - they buy the 3-day package, you
upsell them the 3 month package (more of the same)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 9 --

- supplements - they buy 3 bottles, you upsell them 3
more bottles (more of the same)
- newsletter subscription - they buy a 1 year
subscription, you upsell them a lifetime subscription
(more of the same)
- books -> they buy the main book, you upsell them
the advanced masterclass with secrets not in the
book (more of the same)
- ebook -> they buy the ebook, you upsell them the
physical version of the book, along with DVD’s, audio
CD’s and more
- done-for-you (you buy a book on how to fix your
credit, the first upsell is a program that fixes your
credit for you)
- courses - you buy a course on FB ads, the first
upsell is copy/paste Facebook ads they can use today
to make money
- get results faster - they buy a book about how to
lose weight, you upsell them supplements that will
give them faster results
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 9 --

Make sense?
Three biggest needle-movers on upsells
1. Product selection
2. Price
3. Formatting of the upsell (i.e. video, vs. video/text)
- also includes exit pops, exit offers
Product Selection
- The product selection on the upsell is THE most
important part by far.
42% Anti-aging supplement larger size bottle
12% Fish Oil
8% Probiotic
15% Info Product on “looking younger”
- Main focus should be to get the RIGHT product
before you test anything else. Otherwise you’re
simply trying to boost conversions on a weak offer.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 9 --

- Upsell #1 should be your only focus until that is dialed
in (it accounts for 80% of your upsell AOV)
- shoot for a 20% - 45% conversion rate (a lot of this
depends on the price)
- AOV matters more than conversion rate on upsells
(i.e. choose the product that adds the most AOV)
Price
- Majority of people do NOT ever test price on their
upsells (test on 1st upsell)
- testing for AOV boost, not conversions
- Most people price their upsells too low. Can stretch
them higher than you think
- PPG sample packs $3.95 -> $149 sale
- Russell Brunson book $6.95 -> $297 course
- if an offer is very dialed-in, test higher prices on the
upsell (easiest way to boost AOV)
- If they bought 3 bottles of a supplement on the
front-end, try to sell them 6 more (usually works better
than selling them 3 more)
- Formatting is probably more important than copy
when it comes to the upsell
- video (no text below it)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 9 --

- force them to watch
- longer videos than you think (11-15 minutes)
- example below
Big mistakes I see on upsells
- Wrong product
- Copy is too short
- Don’t sell hard enough (have to go through the full
sales process)
- Acknowledge the purchase they just made, tell them
they’re in the right spot, and that their order is getting
ready (say all this in the first 30 seconds)
- Once you have a format that works, replicate it over
and over (make sure each new product you put out
fits the same mold)
- if you know how to make an ebook ->
supplements upsell work, just keep making ebook ->
supplement offers (different variations of it)
- https://www.paleorecipeteam.com/sweetsoto1j/
